verdict_reasoning: SRC is a non-receptor tyrosine kinase canonically anchored by N-myristoylation to the INNER leaflet of the plasma membrane; it has no transmembrane domain and no outer-leaflet exposure under baseline conditions. However, in cancer cells overexpressing SRC, a well-documented noncanonical pathway — autophagolysosomal exocytosis (ALE) — topologically inverts SRC and presents it on the extracellular face of the plasma membrane, making it a surface antibody target. This outer-leaflet exposure is cell-state-induced (cancer-specific) and occurs via the protein's own lipid anchor undergoing inversion, qualifying as contextual via lysosomal_exocytosis.
